text.content: Tracking recipe experiments for blood sugar management. Rules: everything gets tried twice before it earns a verdict. Rating: Will Make Again / Fine / Never Again.

rich_text.plain_text: Tracking recipe experiments for blood sugar management. Rules: everything gets tried twice before it earns a verdict. Rating: Will Make Again / Fine / Never Again.

text.content: Low-GI Dinner Rotation

rich_text.plain_text: Low-GI Dinner Rotation

text.content: Lemon herb chicken + roasted broccoli: GI low, ~$8.50/serving, 35 min. Solid weeknight go-to. Will Make Again.

rich_text.plain_text: Lemon herb chicken + roasted broccoli: GI low, ~$8.50/serving, 35 min. Solid weeknight go-to. Will Make Again.

text.content: Black bean sweet potato tacos: GI low-medium (sweet potato bumps it), ~$7/serving, 40 min. Good but messy. Fine.

rich_text.plain_text: Black bean sweet potato tacos: GI low-medium (sweet potato bumps it), ~$7/serving, 40 min. Good but messy. Fine.

text.content: Salmon + cauliflower rice bowls with soy ginger sauce: GI low, ~$10/serving, 25 min. The sauce makes it. Will Make Again.

rich_text.plain_text: Salmon + cauliflower rice bowls with soy ginger sauce: GI low, ~$10/serving, 25 min. The sauce makes it. Will Make Again.

text.content: Turkey veggie stir fry (no rice): GI low, ~$8/serving, 30 min. Needs more sauce next time. Fine.

rich_text.plain_text: Turkey veggie stir fry (no rice): GI low, ~$8/serving, 30 min. Needs more sauce next time. Fine.

text.content: Lentil soup + whole grain bread: GI low-medium, ~$5.50/serving, 45 min hands-off. Double batch freezes perfectly. Will Make Again.

rich_text.plain_text: Lentil soup + whole grain bread: GI low-medium, ~$5.50/serving, 45 min hands-off. Double batch freezes perfectly. Will Make Again.

text.content: Glycemic Index Notes

rich_text.plain_text: Glycemic Index Notes

text.content:
Low GI (under 55): most vegetables, lentils, chickpeas, quinoa, cauliflower rice, nuts, most beans
Medium GI (56-69): sweet potatoes, brown rice, whole wheat bread, oats, banana
High GI (70+): white rice, white bread, russet potatoes, most breakfast cereals

Key thing: pair carbs with protein or fat to slow absorption. A sweet potato with chicken hits different than sweet potato alone.

rich_text.plain_text:
Low GI (under 55): most vegetables, lentils, chickpeas, quinoa, cauliflower rice, nuts, most beans
Medium GI (56-69): sweet potatoes, brown rice, whole wheat bread, oats, banana
High GI (70+): white rice, white bread, russet potatoes, most breakfast cereals

Key thing: pair carbs with protein or fat to slow absorption. A sweet potato with chicken hits different than sweet potato alone.

text.content: Meal Prep Log

rich_text.plain_text: Meal Prep Log

text.content: Week of Jan 12: Lemon herb chicken x4, salmon bowls x3, big batch lentil soup. Total grocery: ~$68 H-E-B delivery. About 2 hours Sunday afternoon. Lunches covered Mon-Fri.

rich_text.plain_text: Week of Jan 12: Lemon herb chicken x4, salmon bowls x3, big batch lentil soup. Total grocery: ~$68 H-E-B delivery. About 2 hours Sunday afternoon. Lunches covered Mon-Fri.

text.content: Week of Jan 19: Chicken shawarma bowls x4, black bean quinoa salad x4. Tried the Budget Bytes meal prep recipes. Shawarma was a hit. Quinoa salad held up great by day 4.

rich_text.plain_text: Week of Jan 19: Chicken shawarma bowls x4, black bean quinoa salad x4. Tried the Budget Bytes meal prep recipes. Shawarma was a hit. Quinoa salad held up great by day 4.

text.content: Week of Jan 26: Cod + roasted veggies x3, turkey meatballs x4, leftover lentil soup from freezer. Cod was ok but doesn't reheat well. Stick to salmon for fish prep.

rich_text.plain_text: Week of Jan 26: Cod + roasted veggies x3, turkey meatballs x4, leftover lentil soup from freezer. Cod was ok but doesn't reheat well. Stick to salmon for fish prep.

text.content: Update 2/2: Tested new combos. Chicken shawarma with tahini keeps GI low. Cod reheated in oven (not microwave) stays flaky. Adding chickpeas to anything boosts protein and keeps GI stable. Overnight oats with berries and walnuts = perfect low-GI breakfast, prep 5 bowls on Sunday.

rich_text.plain_text: Update 2/2: Tested new combos. Chicken shawarma with tahini keeps GI low. Cod reheated in oven (not microwave) stays flaky. Adding chickpeas to anything boosts protein and keeps GI stable. Overnight oats with berries and walnuts = perfect low-GI breakfast, prep 5 bowls on Sunday.

text.content: Vet Checklist

rich_text.plain_text: Vet Checklist

text.content:
Standard deduction makes more sense than itemizing. Even with the student loan interest, nowhere close to $30K in deductions.

Student loan interest ($1,412.86) is above-the-line so we get it regardless of standard vs itemized.

Refund goes to Chase checking (4872). Set up direct deposit in TurboTax.

Scheduled TurboTax session for March 22 afternoon. All docs pulled and ready.

text.content: Rough Numbers

rich_text.plain_text: Rough Numbers

text.content:
Combined gross income: ~$108K (me ~$50K + Tasha ~$58K)
Standard deduction: -$30,000
Student loan interest: -$1,412
Taxable income: ~$76,500
Estimated federal tax: ~$9,200
Total withheld (both W-2s combined): ~$11,000
Expected refund: ~$1,800

Not bad. Enough to cover a couple months of student loan payments.

rich_text.plain_text: Fur Baby Project

text.content: Top secret. Tasha keeps saying we can't get a dog because of her shifts, but she lights up every time we're at the dog park. Going to make it happen.

text.content: Breed Research

rich_text.plain_text: Breed Research

text.content: Cavalier King Charles: cute, low energy, good for apartments. Prone to heart issues and expensive from breeders ($1,500+). Pass.

rich_text.plain_text: Cavalier King Charles: cute, low energy, good for apartments. Prone to heart issues and expensive from breeders ($1,500+). Pass.

text.content: Goldendoodle: hypoallergenic-ish, good temperament. Huge and needs tons of grooming. We don't have the yard. Pass.

rich_text.plain_text: Goldendoodle: hypoallergenic-ish, good temperament. Huge and needs tons of grooming. We don't have the yard. Pass.

text.content: Rescue mix: healthiest option, most ethical, way cheaper ($100–350 adoption fee). Contacted BARC Houston. Maya is my person there.

rich_text.plain_text: Rescue mix: healthiest option, most ethical, way cheaper ($100–350 adoption fee). Contacted BARC Houston. Maya is my person there.

text.content: Going rescue. Maya sent photos of a few available dogs in January. One medium mix (lab/pit maybe?) looks perfect — calm energy, already house-trained, 2 years old. Name: Koda.

rich_text.plain_text: Going rescue. Maya sent photos of a few available dogs in January. One medium mix (lab/pit maybe?) looks perfect — calm energy, already house-trained, 2 years old. Name: Koda.

text.content:

• Crate + bed: ~$85
• Food + bowls: ~$60
• Leash, collar, harness: ~$50
• Toys + chews: ~$30
• First vet visit + vaccines: ~$115

Petco run for basics. Vet appointment after adoption.
Charging supplies to Chase checking so it doesn't show up on CC statement Tasha sees.

text.content: Reveal Plan

rich_text.plain_text: Reveal Plan

text.content:
Timeline:
1. Home visit with Maya — she checks the space, I meet Koda (Mar 8, Tasha on shift). Clean house before. HIDE any dog supplies.
2. Confirm adoption Saturday with Maya (call mid-April, make sure Tasha is working that morning)
3. Pick up day: Saturday morning, "running errands" — bring carrier
4. Set up in the living room: bed, toys, little bow
5. Tasha comes home to a fully set up fur baby situation

The face she's going to make. I cannot wait.
